pattern: After locating the rook, shooting a ray in each of the four cardinal directions until the first piece tells you whether that direction yields a capture.
hints: First find where the rook sits, then consider what it meets travelling up, down, left, and right.
In each direction the rook stops at the first non-empty square; only the identity of that square matters.
Walk outward along each of the four directions: if the first piece you hit is a pawn count it, if it is a bishop or the edge it counts for nothing.

edge_inputs.description: rook captures pawns in three open directions
a bishop shields one pawn while others are capturable
rook in the corner with no pieces around
rook in a corner with pawns at the far edges
pawn directly adjacent on each side

stress.description: random 8x8 board with one rook and scattered pawns and bishops
stress.note: exactly one rook placed last so the board is always valid
